files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "temporal-6514-stuck-temporal-server",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "temporal: Stuck Temporal Server",
+    "description": "Stuck Temporal Server. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify temporal troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your temporal deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal -n temporal --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review temporal configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant temporal configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal\nkubectl get configmap -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=temporal\n```\n## Expected Behavior\nThe Temporal server processes workflows normally\n\n## Actual Behavior\nWorkflows are stuck and the server logs indicate the history service is stuck in a loop trying to connect to a Postgres 14.12 DB with `Error: no usable"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Stuck Temporal Server",
+        "description": "## What changed?\nI fixed a bug in our sql `DBHandle` that could cause us to never reconnect to the database when an issue occurs.\n\n## Why?\nWe should only update the last refresh time when we actually refresh. As written we always update the last refresh time even when we don't refresh.\n\nIf _every_\n```yaml\ndriver: bad connection\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Stuck Temporal Server is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal -n temporal --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n temporal --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "## What changed?\nI fixed a bug in our sql `DBHandle` that could cause us to never reconnect to the database when an issue occurs.\n\n## Why?\nWe should only update the last refresh time when we actually refresh. As written we always update the last refresh time even when we don't refresh.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "driver: bad connection"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "temporal",
+      "community",
+      "workflow-engine",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "temporal"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/temporalio/temporal/issues/6514",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/temporalio/temporal",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/temporalio/temporal/pull/6538"
+    },
+    "reactions": 3,
+    "comments": 11,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with temporal installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-19T06:52:23.597Z",
